license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.u3meyk6
title: Callichthyidae (Ostariophysi: Siluriformes) dissection protocol
author: Karina Carvalho F. Ferreira

steps_raw.instruction: Continue the incision in caudal direction to the limit of the celomathic cavity, by passing the pelvic girdle and ending in the point immediately posterior to the pelvic girdle.
Remove adipose tissue with the aidof the thin point fórceps in order to better visualize internal organs.
Release the organs from the peritoneum using the spacer.
Locate the esophagus and make a cross-section on it using the forceps and scissors.
Remove the internal organs from the celomathic cavity with the organs without any damage and keepingall the information about shape and relative position of each organ.
With the organs outside the celomathic cavity, separate the reproductive organs from the digestive system organs.
Remove the connective tissue located between the walls and intertinal loops. Fromthere, the intertinal walls are separated and the digestive tube is distended.
Make a incision in the medial portion of the abdomen at the point immediately posterior to the pectoral girdle.

Extend the opening from the incision towards the left flank in the ventral view, with the aid of the scissors, bypassing the pectoral girdle towards the dorsal portion of the specimens up to the limit of the celomathic cavity.
Lift up the skin and muscles that ventrally with hold internal organs with the aidof the thin point fórceps, and expose the organs.
Disconnect the intestine from the anus using forceps and scissors.
